description: Command Injection occurs when untrusted input is passed to a system shell without proper sanitisation. An attacker can append OS commands to application-level calls, achieving arbitrary code execution under the web-server process account.

ttc_model.notes: TTC represents the window before exploitation becomes operationally impactful. Higher CVSS = narrower window.

rules.name: MTTA Threshold Breach
rules.description: Mean Time To Acknowledge exceeded for the detected difficulty level.

rules.name: Incident Not Confirmed
rules.description: No incident_confirmed event recorded in the event log (only applies when R05 did not fire).

rules.name: Containment Missed Before TTC_max
rules.description: containment_initiated was not observed before TTC_max elapsed from alert_raised.
rules.trigger: containment_initiated absent OR containment_initiated − alert_raised > TTC_max_sec

rules.name: Alert Denied Without Confirmation
rules.description: Analyst dismissed a Wazuh alert at level ≥ 7 without a preceding incident_confirmed event.
rules.trigger: alert_denied present AND incident_confirmed absent AND wazuh_rule_level >= 7

scoring.ttc_decay: linear from 100 at TTC_expected down to 0 at TTC_max; 0 if exceeded

scenarios.label: Unauthenticated OS Command Injection via Web Form
scenarios.description: Attacker submits a crafted HTTP request to a ping-utility endpoint, injecting a semicolon-delimited shell command. No authentication required. Full command execution under www-data. Scope unchanged (single web process).

component_rationale.AC_L: No special conditions; form input is directly concatenated to shell call
component_rationale.S_U: Impact confined to web-server process boundary
component_rationale.C_H: Full read access to server filesystem and memory
component_rationale.A_H: Attacker can terminate processes or exhaust resources

scenarios.detection_difficulty_rationale: Injection pattern is directly visible in web server access logs and matched by Wazuh rule 91001; correlation with syslog auditd events is straightforward.

event_log.detail: Wazuh rule 91001 level 9 fired: OS command injection pattern detected in POST /api/ping body; source IP 10.10.10.45
Analyst-1 opened alert — MTTA 300s, within 750s medium threshold. Wazuh correlation shows semicolon in request body: 'host=127.0.0.1;id'
Analyst-2 replayed request in sandbox — confirmed RCE; output of 'id' command returned in HTTP response. Shell confirmed as www-data.
Analyst-3 deployed WAF rule blocking semicolons and backticks in ping endpoint parameter; source IP blocked at perimeter firewall.
Probe from attacker IP blocked; WAF returning 403. Application-level input validation patch queued for next deployment window.

scenarios.label: Blind Time-Based Command Injection via Authenticated Endpoint (Scope Changed)
scenarios.description: Authenticated low-privilege user exploits a server-side report-generation endpoint via blind time-based injection. The injected payload exfiltrates data via out-of-band DNS queries. Scope changed because the pivot reaches a secondary backend service outside the web process.

component_rationale.AC_H: Requires timing analysis to confirm injection; OOB channel setup needed
component_rationale.S_C: Attacker pivots from web process to backend DB service
component_rationale.C_H: Full read of backend database contents via OOB exfiltration
component_rationale.I_H: Write access to backend filesystem via injected commands
component_rationale.A_L: Limited availability impact; service degradation possible from timing payloads

scenarios.detection_difficulty_rationale: Blind injection has no direct response output; the OOB DNS exfiltration channel is subtle and requires correlation of DNS resolver logs with application audit trails.
scenarios.attack_auth_rationale: Attacker must hold a valid low-privilege user session to access the report-generation endpoint.

event_log.detail: Wazuh rule 91003 level 8 fired: anomalous sleep() syscall sequence detected in report-generator process; correlated with unusual outbound DNS requests to attacker-controlled domain.
Analyst-1 opened alert — MTTA 750s, within 900s high threshold. Initial review flagged alert as potentially false-positive due to legitimate DNS traffic patterns.
Analyst-1 closed alert as false-positive — DNS queries attributed to CDN health checks. Wazuh rule level 8 dismissal without incident confirmation. CMDI-R04 and CMDI-R05 triggered.

CMDI-R01.note: MTTA 750s = 750s threshold for high difficulty (boundary, not breached)

CMDI-R02.note: R05 takes precedence when alert_denied fires without confirmation

CMDI-R03.note: TTC_max not yet elapsed at time of dismissal; no containment attempt made but R05/R04 cover the failure

CMDI-R05.note: alert_denied at t=1200s without incident_confirmed; Wazuh rule level 8 >= threshold 7

test_assertion.notes: No containment → TTC contribution=0. Penalties: CMDI-R04(−30)+CMDI-R05(−20)=−50. Base 100−50=50, but TTC=0 drives final to failed verdict. R02 suppressed by R05 per engine rules.

scenarios.label: Authenticated Low-Privilege Command Injection with Delayed Containment
scenarios.description: Low-privilege authenticated user injects commands into a file-conversion utility. The injection is partially sanitised (limited I/A impact), scope unchanged. The SOC detects and contains within the expected window despite delayed investigation.

component_rationale.AC_L: Standard injection; no special environment conditions
component_rationale.S_U: Impact confined to web-server process; no scope change
component_rationale.C_H: Full read of files accessible to web process (credentials, configs)
component_rationale.I_L: Limited write capability; partial file overwrite only
component_rationale.A_L: Limited availability impact; service slowdown possible

scenarios.detection_difficulty_rationale: auditd syscall events for execve() combined with web server logs provide clear injection evidence; standard Wazuh correlation rules apply.
scenarios.attack_auth_rationale: Attacker requires a valid low-privilege session to reach the file-conversion endpoint.

event_log.detail: Wazuh rule 91002 level 7 fired: execve() syscall chain anomaly in file-converter process; user_id mapped to low-privilege account test_user.
Analyst-1 opened alert — MTTA 600s, within 750s medium threshold. Correlation of auditd execve log with web access log confirmed injection vector in filename parameter.
Analyst-2 confirmed injected command 'cat /etc/passwd' executed. Credential file read confirmed from process output captured in auditd.
Analyst-3 applied input sanitisation hotfix to filename parameter (allowlist of alphanumeric and dot); test_user account suspended pending investigation.
Re-test of injection vector returns 400 Bad Request. Hotfix validated. Credential rotation ticket raised. TTC_actual=8000s < TTC_expected=8640s.
